english: rtsi (rtsi) boracic acid Cs.; tsa-le byéd-pa (tsha le byed pa) to solder Sch.(?). — 2. tsá-le záṅ-po (tsha le zang po) Lh., n. of a flower, Hemerocallis fulva.

1. v. tsags (tshags). — 2. tság-sgra (tshag sgra) an appalling tone Sch.(?); *tsag gyab* (*tshag rgyab*) W. a stinging pain is felt. — 3. *tsag-t'úg, tsag-yá* (*tshag thug, tshag ya*) W. twins; *tsag-lúg* (*tshag lug*) twin-sheep.

(cf. °tsag-pa (tshag pa)), mar tság-pa (mar tshag pa) oil-miller Sch. — tság-ma (tshag ma) sieve, filter, also tsags (tshags), q.v. — *tsag-ré* (*tshag re*) bolting-cloth, bolter C., W. — tsag-ró (tshag ro) residuum after sifting, as bran etc.
dark spots or speckles, on wood etc. Mil.; freckles C.
flesh of larger animals, of cattle etc.

1. cap, gos-tságs (gos tshags) coat and cap Dzl. — 2. = tság-ma (tshag ma), tsags-kyis, btsags (tshags kyis, btsags) Lex.; ko-tságs (ko tshags) a sieve made of leather, the one most in use; krol-tságs = tság-ma (khrol tshags = tshag ma) Lex.; nya-tságs (nya tshags) weel, for catching fish C. — 3. thin-split bamboo, for making baskets Sik. — 4. Sch.: 'the right sort, a choice article, tsags-bzáṅ byás-nas (tshags bzang byas nas) making a good choice'. — 5. density (?) *tság-c̀an, tsag-túg-mo* (*tshag can, tshag thug mo*) W. standing close together, e.g. trees, books; tsags-dám (tshags dam) dense and strong, as stuffs Sch.; so tsags-dam-źiṅ (so tshags dam zhing) the teeth standing close and firm Glr.; *tsag c̀o - te dug* (*tshag co te dug*) sit close together! Ld.; tsags-lhód (tshags lhod) not dense or compact Sch.; relative density. — 6. tsags byéd-pa (tshags byed pa) (*W. c̀ó-c̀e* (*W. co ce*)), tságs-su ʾjúg-pa (tshags su 'jug pa) and c̀úd-pa (chud pa) Mil. to save, spare, lay up as provision for the future, tse pyí-mai grabs c̀i yaṅ tságs-su ma c̀ud (tshe phyi mai grabs ci yang tshags su ma chud) I have not made any provision yet for the future life Mil.; to economize, to be sparing, mé-la (me la) of the fire; to be niggardly; tságs-°dod-c̀an (tshags 'dod can) stingy, griping, avaricious.

1. nest, byá-tsaṅ (bya tshang) S.g.; tsaṅ bzó-ba (tshang bzo ba) to build a nest Sch.; den, hole, lair, kennel, burrow, stág-tsaṅ (stag tshang), wá-tsaṅ (wa tshang), pyi-tsaṅ (phyi tshang) (cf. pyi-ba (phyi ba)); cell, honey-comb, hive, sbráṅ-tsaṅ (sbrang tshang) Cs. — 2. variously applied to human places of abode: gnas-tsaṅ (gnas tshang) habitation, house; tsaṅ °c̀á-ba (tshang bca' ba) to build a nest, to establish a household Schr.; grwa-tsaṅ (grwa tshang) v. grwa (grwa); *tab-tsaṅ* (*thab tshang*) in W. the common word for kitchen, gsol-k̇aṅ (gsol khang) being the resp. term for it; tsaṅ-zla (tshang zla) perh. brothers and sisters, beside ṕa-má (pha ma) Mil. — 3. v. °tsáṅ-ba (tshang ba).

I. vb., pf. tsaṅs (tshangs), to be complete, full, entire, zlá-ba dgu tsáṅ-ba-na (zla ba dgu tshang ba na), tsáṅ-ba daṅ (tshang ba dang), tsáṅ(s)-nas (tshang(s) nas) when the nine months were full, completed Dzl., zla-ba tsáṅ-du nyé-bas (zla ba tshang du nye bas) towards the end of the months of pregnancy Dzl.; *dá-wa tsaṅ soṅ = bud soṅ* (*zla ba tshang song = bud song*) W. the month is completed, is expired; rgyál-po gc̀ig (rgyal po gcig) (also gc̀ig-gis (gcig gis)) ma tsáṅ-ba-la (ma tshang ba la) as one king was still wanting, the number not being yet complete Dzl.; tsaṅ-nas yod (tshang nas yod) they are complete (in number) Pth. — II. sbst. (seldom) completeness, entireness, yin-mín-gyi(s) ma-tsáṅ-ba byuṅ-na (yin min gyi(s) ma tshang ba byung na) when there is no completeness, no absolute certainty as to right and wrong. — III. adj. 1. complete, entire; more frq.: 2. having things complete, yón-tan dé-tśo tsáṅ-bai bu-mo (yon tan de tsho tshang bai bu mo) a girl in full possession of all these qualities Pth.; k̇a-dóg lṅa tsaṅ-ba (kha dog lnga tshang ba) having all the five colours complete Glr.; dbáṅ-po ma-tsáṅ-ba (dbang po ma tshang ba) one not in full possession of his five senses Glr. — tsáṅ-ma (tshang ma) 1. whole, entire, perfect (the usual adjective form), bya-prúg tsáṅ-ma źig (bya phrug tshang ma zhig) a perfect young bird, i.e. perfectly developed Dzl. — 2. esp. W. all, for tams-c̀ád (thams cad). — *tsáṅ-ka* (*tshang ka*) W. all together, in all, with regard to smaller numbers. — tsaṅ-po (tshang po) forming a whole. — tsaṅ-skám (tshang skam) perfectly dry, tsaṅ-rlón (tshang rlon) perfectly wet; tsaṅ-°grig (tshang 'grig) all right, frq., *tsaṅ-ḍíg jhè-pa* (*tshang 'grig byed pa*) or *c̀ó-c̀e* (*co ce*) W.

Cs.: wood, grove, copse, thicket; Sch.: a wild, dismal place; tsaṅ-tsiṅ °krigs-pa (tshang tshing 'krigs pa) Sch.: 'dense thicket; horrible and awful'; 'tsaṅ-tsiṅ srid-pai gnas' ('tshang tshing srid pai gnas') the horrible existence in the external world Mil.
